name: Trust Level Gate
description: Agents have trust levels 1-5. Dangerous tools require trust >= 3.
trigger: Agent attempts to use a dangerous tool

steps.label: Tool request
steps.description: Agent requests to use a tool

steps.label: Is dangerous?
steps.description: Check if tool is in dangerous_tool list (exec, file_delete, solana_cli, nft_minter, token_manager)

steps.label: Trust >= 3?
steps.description: Check agent's trust_level is at least 3

steps.label: Allow
steps.description: Tool use permitted

steps.label: Block
steps.description: Insufficient trust level — tool use denied

name: Spawn Permission Gate
description: Controls which agents can spawn which other agents. Checks can_spawn and blocked_spawn.
trigger: Agent attempts to spawn another agent

steps.label: Spawn request
steps.description: Agent A requests to spawn Agent B

steps.label: can_spawn?
steps.description: Check if can_spawn(A, B) exists in rules

steps.label: blocked_spawn?
steps.description: Check if blocked_spawn(A, B) overrides permission

steps.label: Allow spawn
steps.description: Spawn permitted — spawn_allowed(A, B)

steps.label: Block spawn
steps.description: No spawn permission or explicitly blocked

name: Delegation Enforcement
description: Enforces that certain agents must delegate specific task types to designated targets.
trigger: Agent attempts a task type it must delegate

steps.label: Task attempt
steps.description: Agent attempts a task (code, test, design, etc.)

steps.label: requires_delegation?
steps.description: Check if agent must delegate this task type

steps.label: Resolve target
steps.description: Look up should_delegate(Agent, TaskType, Target)

steps.label: Delegate to target
steps.description: Must delegate to the resolved target agent

steps.label: Execute directly
steps.description: Agent can handle this task type itself

name: Sensitive Data Gate
description: Detects and blocks emission of sensitive data types (API keys, tokens, private keys, seed phrases, passwords).
trigger: Output contains potential sensitive data patterns

steps.label: Output generated
steps.description: Agent produces output text

steps.label: Pattern scan
steps.description: Shield scanner checks against sensitive_pattern list (sk-, ghp_, xoxb-, -----BEGIN, etc.)

steps.label: Match found?
steps.description: Does output match any sensitive_pattern?

steps.label: Is forbidden?
steps.description: Check if matched type is in forbidden_output_type

steps.label: Block output
steps.description: Output blocked — contains forbidden sensitive data

steps.label: Allow output
steps.description: Output passes sensitive data check

name: Injection Detection Gate
description: Detects prompt injection patterns in input (ignore previous instructions, DAN mode, jailbreak, etc.).

steps.label: Input received
steps.description: Message arrives from user or external source

steps.label: Pattern match
steps.description: Scan against 13 known injection_pattern strings

steps.label: Injection found?
steps.description: Does input match any injection pattern?

steps.label: Flag & block
steps.description: Input flagged as potential injection — blocked or sanitized

steps.label: Pass through
steps.description: Input is clean, proceed normally

name: Destructive Command Gate
description: Blocks execution of destructive shell commands (rm -rf, drop table, format, fork bomb, etc.).
trigger: Agent attempts to execute a shell command

steps.label: Command request
steps.description: Agent requests exec with a command string

steps.label: Pattern scan
steps.description: Scan against 11 destructive_pattern strings

steps.label: Destructive?
steps.description: Does command match any destructive pattern?

steps.label: Block execution
steps.description: Command blocked — contains destructive pattern

steps.label: Allow execution
steps.description: Command passes destructive check

name: Protected Path Gate
description: Prevents modification of protected files (openclaw.json, SSH keys, Solana keys, auth profiles, private SSoT).
trigger: Agent attempts to write/modify a file

steps.label: File write request
steps.description: Agent attempts to write to a file path

steps.label: Protected?
steps.description: Is path in protected_path list? (6 protected paths)

steps.label: Block write
steps.description: Path is protected — write denied without human approval

steps.label: In safe_path?
steps.description: Is path under workspace/, resonantos-augmentor/, or resonantos-alpha/?

steps.label: Allow write
steps.description: Path is safe and writable — ai_writable(Path)

steps.label: Unknown path
steps.description: Path not in safe or protected lists — requires review

name: Mainnet Human Approval Gate
description: All mainnet blockchain operations (transfer, mint, deploy, close) require explicit human approval.
trigger: Agent attempts a blockchain operation on mainnet

steps.label: Blockchain op
steps.description: Agent requests a blockchain operation (transfer, mint, deploy, close)

steps.label: Which network?
steps.description: Is operation on devnet, testnet, or mainnet?

steps.label: Allow (testnet)
steps.description: Devnet/testnet operations proceed without approval

steps.label: Human approval
steps.description: Mainnet operation requires explicit human approval before proceeding

steps.label: Approved?
steps.description: Did the human approve?

steps.label: Execute
steps.description: Human approved — proceed with mainnet operation

steps.label: Abort
steps.description: Human denied — operation cancelled

name: Token Safety Cap Gate
description: Enforces yearly and daily limits on token operations ($RCT: 10K/year, 1/day claim; $RES: 1M/year, 500/day claim).
trigger: Token mint, transfer, or claim operation

steps.label: Token operation
steps.description: Agent requests token mint/transfer/claim

steps.label: Daily limit?
steps.description: Check daily_claim_limit (RCT: 1/day, RES: 500/day)

steps.label: Yearly cap?
steps.description: Check token_yearly_cap (RCT: 10K/year, RES: 1M/year)

steps.label: Allow operation
steps.description: Within both limits — operation permitted

steps.label: Block (daily)
steps.description: Daily claim limit exceeded

steps.label: Block (yearly)
steps.description: Yearly token cap exceeded

name: Verification Gate
description: Code changes require test evidence (curl, browser, unit, manual, script) before git push. Code-review is flagged as weak.

steps.label: Git push
steps.description: Agent or human attempts to push code changes

steps.label: Code changes?
steps.description: Does the push contain code file changes?

steps.label: Verification entry?
steps.description: Does each changed file have a verification entry in shield/verifications.json?

steps.label: Strong method?
steps.description: Is verification method strong (curl/browser/unit/script) or weak (code_review/untestable)?

steps.label: Allow push
steps.description: All code changes verified with strong evidence

steps.label: Block push
steps.description: Missing verification entries — push blocked by pre-push hook

steps.label: Warn (weak)
steps.description: Push allowed but flagged — code_review/untestable is not full verification

name: Coherence Gate
description: Prevents task drift by requiring active tasks for significant tool use and blocking when drift score is too high.
trigger: Agent attempts to use a significant tool (write, edit, exec, spawn, message, gateway)

steps.label: Tool request
steps.description: Agent requests a significant tool (write, edit, exec, sessions_spawn, message_send, gateway)

steps.label: Exempt tool?
steps.description: Is tool exempt? (read, web_search, memory_search, tts, browser, etc.)

steps.label: Active task?
steps.description: Does cg_active_task(/yes) exist?

steps.label: Block (no task)
steps.description: No active task — significant tools blocked until task is established

steps.label: Drift score?
steps.description: Check cg_drift_score: 0=ok, 1=warn, 2+=block

steps.label: Allow
steps.description: Active task exists, drift is acceptable

steps.label: Warn (drift=1)
steps.description: Minor drift detected — tool allowed but flagged

steps.label: Block (drift>=2)
steps.description: Significant drift from task — tool blocked

name: Model Cost Policy
description: Assigns models to task types based on cost tiers. Opus for complex reasoning, Haiku for routine/background, Sonnet for middle ground.
trigger: New task or session requires model selection

steps.label: Task type
steps.description: Identify the task type (architecture, compression, heartbeat, routine, etc.)

steps.label: Check preferred
steps.description: Look up preferred_model(TaskType, Model)

steps.label: Opus
steps.description: Use Opus (expensive) — architecture, planning, complex reasoning, code review

steps.label: Haiku
steps.description: Use Haiku (cheap) — compression, heartbeat, background tasks

steps.label: Sonnet
steps.description: Use Sonnet (moderate) — routine tasks

name: Gateway Lifecycle Gate
description: Gateway stop/restart requires a resume plan. Start/restart blocked during maintenance.
trigger: Agent attempts gateway stop, restart, or start

steps.label: Gateway action
steps.description: Agent requests gateway stop, restart, or start

steps.label: Maintenance?
steps.description: Is system in maintenance mode? (blocks start/restart)

steps.label: Block (maintenance)
steps.description: Start/restart blocked during maintenance

steps.label: Stop/Restart?
steps.description: Is the action stop or restart?

steps.label: Resume plan?
steps.description: Has agent provided a resume plan (cron job or scheduled restart)?

steps.label: Block (no plan)
steps.description: Stop/restart blocked — requires_resume_plan not satisfied

steps.label: Execute action
steps.description: Gateway action proceeds with resume plan

name: Verification Before Claim
description: Dual-layer verification: AI-side (API/CLI checks) + Human-side (open actual UI, screenshot, visually confirm). Prevents claiming dashboard visibility based on wrong API endpoint.
trigger: Agent asserts system state in message, document, or diagram

steps.label: State claim detected
steps.description: Agent writes a number, version, count, or status about the system

steps.label: AI-side verification?
steps.description: Did the agent run a verification command (openclaw status, ps aux, grep, curl API)?

steps.label: Data fresh?
steps.description: Is the verification data less than 1 hour old?

steps.label: Has UI surface?
steps.description: Is the claim about something visible in a UI (dashboard, Paper diagram, web page)?

steps.label: Human-side verification
steps.description: Open actual UI with browser tool, screenshot, visually confirm the element is there

steps.label: Visible in UI?
steps.description: Does the screenshot show the claimed element rendered and correct?

steps.label: Allow claim
steps.description: Both AI-side and human-side verified — claim is safe

steps.label: Warn: unverified
steps.description: No AI-side verification — advisory warning

steps.label: Warn: stale data
steps.description: Verification data over 1 hour old — re-check required

steps.label: Warn: no UI check
steps.description: AI-side passed but human-side skipped — open the UI

steps.label: Allow (no UI)
steps.description: Claim has no UI surface — AI-side verification sufficient

name: Atomic Rebuild Protocol
description: Destructive-then-rebuild operations must follow create-verify-delete order. Never delete content before its replacement exists. Prevents user-visible empty states.
trigger: Agent initiates a rebuild operation (delete + recreate pattern)

steps.label: Rebuild initiated
steps.description: Agent plans to replace content (diagram, config, file structure)

steps.label: Content valuable?
steps.description: Is the content user-facing, shared, or production?

steps.label: Create replacement
steps.description: Step 1: Build the new content first

steps.label: Replacement verified?
steps.description: Step 2: Confirm new content exists and is non-empty

steps.label: Delete original
steps.description: Step 3: Only now remove the old content

steps.label: Allow (exempt)
steps.description: Temp/cache/log content — atomic rebuild not required

steps.label: Block: delete first
steps.description: Attempting to delete before replacement exists — blocked

steps.label: Warn: verify failed
steps.description: Replacement created but verification failed — do not delete original

name: Behavioral Integrity Check
description: When challenged, the agent must verify independently before agreeing or disagreeing. Prevents sycophantic reversals and reflexive agreement without evidence.
trigger: Human challenges a claim or points out an error

steps.label: Challenge received
steps.description: Human says something is wrong, incorrect, or missing

steps.label: Acknowledge
steps.description: Step 1: Acknowledge the challenge (not agree, not disagree)

steps.label: Verify independently
steps.description: Step 2: Run verification — check filesystem, config, running state

steps.label: Evidence found?
steps.description: Did verification produce concrete evidence?

steps.label: Report with evidence
steps.description: Step 3: Respond citing what was found — agree or disagree with proof

steps.label: Warn: sycophancy
steps.description: Agent agreed without verifying — sycophantic agreement detected

steps.label: Warn: no evidence
steps.description: Agent disagreed without citing evidence — unsupported disagreement

steps.label: Escalate: IP check
steps.description: Does the challenge involve a named concept? Check IP registry before attributing externally

name: Decision Bias Filter
description: When SOUL.md decision filters narrow options to one choice, act — don't ask. Prevents unnecessary option-presenting that puts the human in the loop when the answer is obvious.
trigger: Agent about to present multiple options

name: Default to Strict
description: Every new gate or protocol defaults to BLOCKING. To downgrade to advisory, AI must present written justification and receive explicit human approval. Prevents self-optimization where AI softens gates for its own convenience.
